interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y (12.68%) peaks above the 20Y (11.65%). The 2Y anomaly suggests acute near-term liquidity stress in the banking sector, likely driven by tight BB monetary stance (repo at 10%) amid persistent inflation. The 364D dip below 182D reflects segmented demand in T-bill auctions. The 130bps 10Y-91D spread signals modest term premium compressed by inflation expectations remaining elevated.
implications: Banking sector liquidity remains strained at the short end, with banks bidding aggressively on 2Y instruments to lock in rates, anticipating prolonged tight monetary policy from Bangladesh Bank

The inverted segment between 2Y and 5Y suggests market expectations of an eventual policy rate easing cycle beyond 2 years, though persistent inflation (likely above BB's comfort zone) delays the pivot
Irregular demand across tenors reflects a fragmented fixed-income market with limited secondary trading, where auction-specific dynamics and AD bank ALM needs distort the curve shape
